div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Roman law cuts the world in two:
things that can be owned / things that cannot

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
res nullius
Belongs to no one yet
Available by first possession
Wild animals, abandoned property

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
res communes omnium
Common to all by natural law
Cannot be privatized
Air, running water, the sea

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Roman law has no name
for this third case

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
הפקר (hefker)
Ownerless in the constitutive sense
Not unclaimed — necessarily unenclosed
The wilderness of Sinai

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Torah given there because
the giving required ground
no one had yet enclosed
(Bamidbar Rabbah 1:7)

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Anyone willing to become
hefker like the wilderness
could receive what was given there

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Default AI data practice:
treat user data as res nullius —
capture by the first processor

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Leo XIV MH ¶ 178:
personal data should be
res communes omnium —
a true common good

div.style: color: rgb(245, 240, 232) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Deeper claim: personal data
participates in personhood —
outside the law of things entirely;
the lawyer must stop before
the first cut
